nodes.name: 曼哈頓
nodes.summary: 曼哈頓是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括红尾鵟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Helm Identification Guides
nodes.summary: Helm Identification Guides是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括红尾鵟、Siberian nuthatch、Yellow-throated warbler，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Pale Male
nodes.summary: Pale Male是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括红尾鵟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 红尾鵟
nodes.summary: 红尾鵟（Red-tailed hawk，学名 Buteo jamaicensis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联3个分布地点节点。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Unlike some other raptors, the red-tailed hawk is seemingly unfazed by considerable human activity and can nest and live in close proximity to large numbers of humans. Thus, the species can also be found in cities, where common prey such as rock pigeons and brown rats may support their populations. One famous urban red-tailed hawk, known as "Pale Male", became the subject of a nonfiction book, Red-Tails in Love: A Wildlife Drama in Central Park, and is the first known red-tail in decades to successfully nest and raise young in the crowded New York City borough of Manhattan.Pale Male – Introduction – Red-tailed Hawk in New York City |Nature

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Unlike some other raptors, the red-tailed hawk is seemingly unfazed by considerable human activity and can nest and live in close proximity to large numbers of humans. Thus, the species can also be found in cities, where common prey such as rock pigeons and brown rats may support their populations. One famous urban red-tailed hawk, known as "Pale Male", became the subject of a nonfiction book, Red-Tails in Love: A Wildlife Drama in Central Park, and is the first known red-tail in decades to successfully nest and raise young in the crowded New York City borough of Manhattan.Pale Male – Introduction – Red-tailed Hawk in New York City |Nature

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The species is a partial migrant, as in most of their range in Canada and Alaska, they almost entirely vacate their breeding grounds.{{cite book |year=2001 |title=Raptors of the World | publisher=Christopher Helm |location=London |isbn=978-0-7136-8026-3 |author1=Ferguson-Lees, J
